entities.name: RICE
entities.description: Wetland rice crop affected by iron toxicity in tropical regions

entities.name: IRON TOXICITY
entities.description: A physiological disorder caused by excess iron mobilized in soil solution under submerged conditions

entities.name: SUBMERGED CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environmental state in wetland rice fields that leads to iron mobilization

entities.name: TROPICAL REGIONS
entities.description: Humid zones where iron toxicity is common

entities.name: ACID SULFATE SOILS
entities.description: Type of soil prone to iron toxicity

entities.name: ACID ULTISOLS
entities.description: Type of soil prone to iron toxicity

entities.name: OXISOLS
entities.description: Type of soil prone to iron toxicity

entities.name: SANDY SOILS
entities.description: Type of soil prone to iron toxicity

entities.name: LOW CATION EXCHANGE CAPACITY
entities.description: Soil property that increases susceptibility to iron toxicity

entities.name: HIGH ACIDITY
entities.description: Soil property that increases susceptibility to iron toxicity

entities.name: REDUCIBLE IRON
entities.description: Soil property that increases susceptibility to iron toxicity

entities.name: ORGANIC MATTER
entities.description: Soil component that influences the occurrence of iron toxicity

entities.name: RICE YIELD
entities.description: The productivity of rice, which can be reduced by 12-100% due to iron toxicity

entities.name: IRON TOLERANCE
entities.description: The genetic ability of rice genotypes to withstand iron toxicity

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY
entities.description: A factor that influences the impact of iron toxicity on rice yields

entities.name: NUTRIENT MANAGEMENT
entities.description: Practices involving balanced nutrient application to manage iron toxicity

entities.name: INTEGRATED MANAGEMENT
entities.description: An approach combining iron-tolerant rice genotypes with balanced nutrient management to increase production
